\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n29: )\n30: \n31: // NodeAbstractResourceInstance represents a resource instance with no\n32: // associated operations. It embeds NodeAbstractResource but additionally\n33: // contains an instance key, used to identify one of potentially many\n34: // instances that were created from a resource in configuration, e.g. using\n35: // the \"count\" or \"for_each\" arguments.\n36: type NodeAbstractResourceInstance struct {\n37: \tNodeAbstractResource\n38: \tAddr addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n39: \n40: \t// These are set via the AttachState method.\n41: \tinstanceState *states.ResourceInstance\n42: \n43: \tDependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\n44: \n45: \tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n46: \n47: \t// During import we may generate configuration for a resource, which needs\n48: \t// to be stored in the final change.\n49: \tgeneratedConfigHCL string\n50: \n51: \t// override is set by the graph itself, just before this node executes.\n52: \toverride *configs.Override\n53: }\n54: \n55: // NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance creates an abstract resource instance graph\n56: // node for the given absolute resource instance address.\n57: func NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) *NodeAbstractResourceInstance {\n58: \t// Due to the fact that we embed NodeAbstractResource, the given address\n59: \t// actually ends up split between the resource address in the embedded\n60: \t// object and the InstanceKey field in our own struct. The\n61: \t// ResourceInstanceAddr method will stick these back together again on\n62: \t// request.\n63: \tr := NewNodeAbstractResource(addr.ContainingResource().Config())\n64: \treturn &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n65: \t\tNodeAbstractResource: *r,\n66: \t\tAddr:                 addr,\n67: \t}\n68: }\n69: \n70: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) Name() string {\n71: \treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String()\n72: }\n73: \n74: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n75: \treturn n.Addr.Module\n76: }\n77: \n78: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) HookResourceIdentity() HookResourceIdentity {\n79: \treturn HookResourceIdentity{\n80: \t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n81: \t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n82: \t}\n83: }\n84: \n85: // GraphNodeReferenceable\n86: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable {\n87: \taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n88: \treturn []addrs.Referenceable{\n89: \t\taddr.Resource,\n90: \n91: \t\t// A resource instance can also be referenced by the address of its\n92: \t\t// containing resource, so that e.g. a reference to aws_instance.foo\n93: \t\t// would match both aws_instance.foo[0] and aws_instance.foo[1].\n94: \t\taddr.ContainingResource().Resource,\n95: \t}\n96: }\n97: \n98: // GraphNodeReferencer\n99: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n100: \t// If we have a configuration attached then we'll delegate to our\n101: \t// embedded abstract resource, which knows how to extract dependencies\n102: \t// from configuration. If there is no config, then the dependencies will\n103: \t// be connected during destroy from those stored in the state.\n104: \tif n.Config != nil {\n105: \t\tif n.Schema == nil {\n106: \t\t\t// We'll produce a log message about this out here so that\n107: \t\t\t// we can include the full instance address, since the equivalent\n108: \t\t\t// message in NodeAbstractResource.References cannot see it.\n109: \t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no schema is attached to %s, so config references cannot be detected\", n.Name())\n110: \t\t\treturn nil\n111: \t\t}\n112: \t\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.References()\n113: \t}\n114: \n115: \t// If we have neither config nor state then we have no references.\n116: \treturn nil\n117: }\n118: \n119: // StateDependencies returns the dependencies which will be saved in the state\n120: // for managed resources, or the most current dependencies for data resources.\n121: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) StateDependencies() []addrs.ConfigResource {\n122: \t// Managed resources prefer the stored dependencies, to avoid possible\n123: \t// conflicts in ordering when refactoring configuration.\n124: \tif s := n.instanceState; s != nil {\n125: \t\tif s.Current != nil {\n126: \t\t\treturn s.Current.Dependencies\n127: \t\t}\n128: \t}\n129: \n130: \t// If there are no stored dependencies, this is either a newly created\n131: \t// managed resource, or a data source, and we can use the most recently\n132: \t// calculated dependencies.\n133: \treturn n.Dependencies\n134: }\n135: \n136: // GraphNodeResourceInstance\n137: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ResourceInstanceAddr() addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n138: \treturn n.Addr\n139: }\n140: \n141: // GraphNodeAttachResourceState\n142: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) AttachResourceState(s *states.Resource) {\n143: \tif s == nil {\n144: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] attaching nil state to %s\", n.Addr)\n145: \t\treturn\n146: \t}\n147: \tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.AttachResourceState for %s\", n.Addr)\n148: \tn.instanceState = s.Instance(n.Addr.Resource.Key)\n149: \tn.storedProviderConfig = s.ProviderConfig\n150: }\n151: \n152: // GraphNodeOverridable\n153: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) ConfigProvider() addrs.AbsProviderConfig {\n154: \treturn \n162: // readDiff returns the planned change for a particular resource instance\n163: // object.\n164: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) readDiff(ctx EvalContext, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, error) {\n165: \tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n166: \taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n167: \n168: \tschema, _ := \tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readDiff: Read %s change from plan for %s\", change.Action, n.Addr)\n177: \n178: \treturn change, nil\n179: }\n180: \n181: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) checkPreventDestroy(change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n182: \tif change == nil || n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n183: \t\treturn nil\n184: \t}\n185: \n186: *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) preApplyHook(ctx EvalContext, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n207: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n208: \n209: \tif change == nil {\n210: \t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"preApplyHook for %s called with nil Change\", n.Addr))\n211: \t}\n212: \n213: \t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\n214: \tif EvalContext, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, err error) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n231: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n232: \n233: \t// Only managed resources have user-visible apply actions.\n234: \tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.ManagedResourceMode {\n235: \t\tvar newState cty.Value\n236: \t\tif state != nil {\n237: \t\t\tnewState = state.Value\n238: \t\t} else \tworkingState phaseState = iota\n253: \trefreshState\n254: \tprevRunState\n255: )\n256: \n257: //go:generate go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/stringer -type phaseState\n258: \n259: // writeResourceInstanceState saves the given object as the current object for\n260: // the selected resource instance.\n261: //\n262: // dependencies is a parameter, instead of those directly attacted to the\n263: // NodeAbstractResourceInstance, because we don't write dependencies for\n264: // datasources.\n265: //\n266: // targetState determines which context state we're writing to during plan. The\n267: // default is the global working state.\n268: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n269: \treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, states.NotDeposed, obj, targetState)\n270: }\n271: \n272: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n273: \tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n274: \t\t// Bail out to avoid silently doing something other than what the\n275: \t\t// caller seems to have intended.\n276: \t\tpanic(\"trying to write current state object using writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\")\n277: \t}\n278: \treturn n.writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx, deposedKey, obj, targetState)\n279: }\n280: \n281: // (this is the private common body of both writeResourceInstanceState and\n282: // writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed. Don't call it directly; instead, use\n283: // one of the two wrappers to be explicit about which of the instance's\n284: // objects you are intending to write.\n285: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeResourceInstanceStateImpl(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, obj *states.ResourceInstanceObject, targetState phaseState) error {\n286: \tabsAddr := n.Addr\n287: \t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n288: \tif err != nil {\n289: \t\treturn err\n290: \t}\n291: \tlogFuncName := \"NodeAbstractResouceInstance.writeResourceInstanceState\"\n292: \tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n293: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr)\n294: \t} else {\n295: \t\tlogFuncName = \"NodeAbstractResouceInstance.writeResourceInstanceStateDeposed\"\n296: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s to %s for %s (deposed key %s)\", logFuncName, targetState, absAddr, deposedKey)\n297: \t}\n298: \n299: \tvar state *states.SyncState\n300: \tswitch targetState {\n301: \tcase workingState:\n302: \t\tstate = ctx.State()\n303: \tcase refreshState:\n304: \t\tstate = ctx.RefreshState()\n305: \tcase prevRunState:\n306: \t\tstate = ctx.PrevRunState()\n307: \tdefault:\n308: \t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unsupported phaseState value %#v\", targetState))\n309: \t}\n310: \tif state == nil {\n311: \t\t// Should not happen, because we shouldn't ever try to write to\n312: \t\t// a state that isn't applicable to the current operation.\n313: \t\t// (We can also get in here for unit tests which are using\n314: \t\t// EvalContextMock but not populating PrevRunStateState with\n315: \t\t// a suitable state object.)\n316: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"state of type %s is not applicable to the current operation; this is a bug in Terraform\", targetState)\n317: \t}\n318: \n319: \t// In spite of the name, this function also handles the non-deposed case\n320: \t// via the writeResourceInstanceState wrapper, by setting deposedKey to\n321: \t// the NotDeposed value (the zero value of DeposedKey).\n322: \tvar write func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc)\n323: \tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n324: \t\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\n325: \t\t\tstate.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(absAddr, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\n326: \t\t}\n327: \t} else {\n328: \t\twrite = func(src *states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc) {\n329: \t\t\tstate.SetResourceInstanceDeposed(absAddr, deposedKey, src, n.ResolvedProvider)\n330: \t\t}\n331: \t}\n332: \n333: \tif obj == nil || obj.Value.IsNull() {\n334: \t\t// No need to encode anything: we'll just write it directly.\n335: \t\twrite(nil)\n336: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s: removing state object for %s\", logFuncName, absAddr)\n337: \t\treturn nil\n338: \t}\n339: \n340: \tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] %s: writing state object for %s\", logFuncName, absAddr)\n341: \n342: \tschema, currentVersion := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(absAddr.ContainingResource().Resource)\n343: \tif schema == nil {\n344: \t\t// It shouldn't be possible to get this far in any real scenario\n345: \t\t// without a schema, but we might end up here in contrived tests that\n346: \t\t// fail to set up their world properly.\n347: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode %s in state: no resource type schema available\", absAddr)\n348: \t}\n349: \n350: \tsrc, err := obj.Encode(schema.ImpliedType(), currentVersion)\n351: \tif err != nil {\n352: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to encode %s in state: %s\", absAddr, err)\n353: \t}\n354: \n355: \twrite(src)\n356: \treturn nil\n357: }\n358: \n359: // planDestroy returns a plain destroy diff.\n360: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) planDestroy(ctx EvalContext, currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n361: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n362: \tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n363: \tvar plan *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n364: \n365: \tabsAddr := n.Addr\n366: \tdeferralAllowed := needed\n382: \t\t// vs. that something being entirely excluded e.g. due to -target.\n383: \t\tnoop := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n384: \t\t\tAddr:        absAddr,\n385: \t\t\tPrevRunAddr: \t\t\t\tAfter:  cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n391: \t\t\t},\n392: \t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n393: \t\t}\n394: \t\treturn noop, deferred, nil\n395: \t}\n396: \n397: \t// If we are {\n423: \t\t// If we have an overridden value from the test framework, that means\n424: \t\t// this value was created without consulting the provider previously.\n425: \t\t// We can just set the \t\t\tTypeName:         n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n435: \t\t\tConfig:           nullVal,\n436: \t\t\tPriorState:       unmarkedPriorVal,\n437: \t\t\tProposedNewState: nullVal,\n438: \t\t\tPriorPrivate:     currentState.Private,\n439: \t\t\tProviderMeta:     metaConfigVal,\n440: \t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n441: \t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n442: \t\t\t},\n443: \t\t})\n444: \t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n445: \n446: \t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n447: \t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n448: \t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n449: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n450: \t\t}\n451: \n452: \t\t// We may not have a config for all destroys, but we want to reference\n453: \t\t// it in the diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\n458: \t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n459: \t\t\treturn plan, deferred, diags\n460: \t\t}\n461: \n462: \t\t// Check that the provider returned a null value here, since that is the\n463: \t\t// only valid value for a destroy plan.\n464: \t\tif !resp.PlannedState.IsNull() && deferred == nil {\n465: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n466: \t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n467: \t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n468: \t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n469: \t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned a non-null destroy value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n470: \t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr),\n471: \t\t\t),\n472: \t\t\t)\n473: \t\t\treturn plan, deferred, diags\n474: \t\t}\n475: \t}\n476: \n477: \t// Plan is always the same for a destroy.\n478: \tplan = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n479: \t\tAddr:        absAddr,\n480: \t\tPrevRunAddr: \t\t},\n487: \t\tPrivate:      resp.PlannedPrivate,\n488: \t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n489: \t}\n490: \n491: \treturn plan, deferred, diags\n492: }\n493: \n494: // planForget returns a Forget change.\n495: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) planForget(ctx EvalContext, currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, currentState.Value,\n533: \t\t\tAfter:  nullVal,\n534: \t\t},\n535: \t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n536: \t}\n537: \n538: \treturn plan, diags\n539: }\n540: \n541: // writeChange saves a planned change for an instance object into the set of\n542: // global planned changes.\n543: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) writeChange(ctx EvalContext, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, deposedKey states.DeposedKey) error {\n544: \tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n545: \n546: \tif change == nil {\n547: \t\t// Caller sets nil to indicate that we need to remove a change from\n548: \t\t// the set of changes.\n549: \t\tchanges.RemoveResourceInstanceChange(n.Addr, deposedKey)\n550: \t\treturn nil\n551: \t}\n552: \n553: \tif change.Addr.String() != n.Addr.String() || change.DeposedKey != deposedKey {\n554: \t\t// Should never happen, and indicates a bug in the caller.\n555: \t\tpanic(\"inconsistent address and/or deposed key in writeChange\")\n556: \t}\n557: \tif change.PrevRunAddr.Resource.Resource.Type == \"\" {\n558: \t\t// Should never happen, and indicates a bug in the caller.\n559: \t\t// (The change.Encode function actually has its own fixup to just\n560: \t\t// quietly make this match change.Addr in the incorrect case, but we\n561: \t\t// intentionally panic here in order to catch incorrect callers where\n562: \t\t// the stack trace will hopefully be actually useful. The tolerance\n563: \t\t// at the next layer down is mainly to accommodate sloppy input in\n564: \t\t// older tests.)\n565: \t\tpanic(\"unpopulated ResourceInstanceChange.PrevRunAddr in writeChange\")\n566: \t}\n567: \n568: \tchanges.AppendResourceInstanceChange(change)\n569: \tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n570: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] writeChange: recorded %s change for %s\", change.Action, n.Addr)\n571: \t} else {\n572: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] writeChange: recorded %s change for %s deposed object %s\", change.Action, n.Addr, deposedKey)\n573: \t}\n574: \n575: \treturn nil\n576: }\n577: \n578: // refresh does a refresh for a resource\n579: // if the second return value is non-nil, the refresh is deferred\n580: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) refresh(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deferralAllowed bool) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n581: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n582: \tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n583: \tabsAddr := n.Addr\n584: \tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n585: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.refresh for %s\", absAddr)\n586: \t} else {\n587: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.refresh for %s (deposed object %s)\", absAddr, deposedKey)\n588: \t}\n589: \tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n590: \tif err != nil {\n591: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags.Append(err)\n592: \t}\n593: \t// If we have no state, we don't do any refreshing\n594: \tif state == nil {\n595: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] refresh: %s: no state, so not refreshing\", absAddr)\n596: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n597: \t}\n598: \n599: \tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\n600: \tif schema == nil {\n601: \t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n602: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type))\n603: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n604: \t}\n605: \n606: \tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\n607: \tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\n608: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n609: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n610: \t}\n611: \n612: \t// Call pre-refresh hook\n613: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n614: \t\treturn h.PreRefresh(n.HookResourceIdentity(), deposedKey, state.Value)\n615: \t}))\n616: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n617: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n618: \t}\n619: \n620: \t// Refresh!\n621: \tpriorVal := state.Value\n622: \n623: \t// Unmarked before sending to provider\n624: \tvar priorMarks []cty.PathValueMarks\n625: \tpriorVal, priorMarks = priorVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n626: \n627: \tvar resp providers.ReadResourceResponse\n628: \tif n.override != nil {\n629: \t\t// If we have an override set for this resource, we don't want to talk\n630: \t\t// to the provider so we'll just return whatever was in state.\n631: \t\tresp = providers.ReadResourceResponse{\n632: \t\t\tNewState: priorVal,\n633: \t\t}\n634: \t} else {\n635: \t\tresp = provider.ReadResource(providers.ReadResourceRequest{\n636: \t\t\tTypeName:     n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n637: \t\t\tPriorState:   priorVal,\n638: \t\t\tPrivate:      state.Private,\n639: \t\t\tProviderMeta: metaConfigVal,\n640: \t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n641: \t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n642: \t\t\t},\n643: \t\t})\n644: \n645: \t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n646: \t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n647: \t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n648: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n649: \t\t}\n650: \n651: \t\tif resp.Deferred != nil {\n652: \t\t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n653: \t\t}\n654: \t}\n655: \tif n.Config != nil {\n656: \t\tresp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String())\n657: \t}\n658: \n659: \tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\n660: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n661: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n662: \t}\n663: \n664: \tif resp.NewState == cty.NilVal {\n665: \t\t// This ought not to happen in real cases since it's not possible to\n666: \t\t// send NilVal over the plugin RPC channel, but it can come up in\n667: \t\t// tests due to sloppy mocking.\n668: \t\tpanic(\"new state is cty.NilVal\")\n669: \t}\n670: \n671: \t// If we have deferred the refresh, we expect the new state not to be wholly known\n672: \t// and callers should be prepared to handle this.\n673: \tif !resp.NewState.IsWhollyKnown() && deferred == nil {\n674: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n675: \t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n676: \t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid object\",\n677: \t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n678: \t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s during refresh: %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n679: \t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, absAddr, \"The returned state contains unknown values\",\n680: \t\t\t),\n681: \t\t))\n682: \t}\n683: \n684: \tfor _, err := range resp.NewState.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\n685: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n686: \t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n687: \t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid object\",\n688: \t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n689: \t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s during refresh: %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n690: \t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, absAddr, tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n691: \t\t\t),\n692: \t\t))\n693: \t}\n694: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n695: \t\treturn state, deferred, diags\n696: \t}\n697: \n698: \tnewState := objchange.NormalizeObjectFromLegacySDK(resp.NewState, schema)\n699: \tif !newState.RawEquals(resp.NewState) {\n700: \t\t// We had to fix up this object in some way, and we still need to\n701: \t\t// accept any changes for compatibility, so all we can do is log a\n702: \t\t// warning about the change.\n703: \t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Provider %q produced an invalid new value containing null blocks for %q during refresh\\n\", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr)\n704: \t}\n705: \n706: \tret := state.DeepCopy()\n707: \tret.Value = newState\n708: \tret.Private = resp.Private\n709: \n710: \t// We have no way to exempt provider using the legacy SDK from this check,\n711: \t// so we can only log inconsistencies with the updated state values.\n712: \t// In most cases these are not errors anyway, and represent \"drift\" from\n713: \t// external changes which will be handled by the subsequent plan.\n714: \tif errs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, priorVal, ret.Value); len(errs) > 0 {\n715: \t\tvar buf strings.Builder\n716: \t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"[WARN] Provider %q produced an unexpected new value for %s during refresh.\", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(), absAddr)\n717: \t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n718: \t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"\\n      - %s\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\n719: \t\t}\n720: \t\tlog.Print(buf.String())\n721: \t}\n722: \n723: \t// Call post-refresh hook\n724: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n725: \t\treturn h.PostRefresh(n.HookResourceIdentity(), deposedKey, priorVal, ret.Value)\n726: \t}))\n727: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n728: \t\treturn ret, deferred, diags\n729: \t}\n730: \n731: \t// Mark the value with any prior marks from the state, and the marks from\n732: \t// the schema. This ensures we capture any marks from the last\n733: \t// configuration, as well as any marks from the schema which were not in\n734: \t// the prior state. New marks may appear when the prior state was from an\n735: \t// import operation, or if the provider added new marks to the schema.\n736: \tret.Value = ret.Value.MarkWithPaths(priorMarks)\n737: \tif moreSensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(ret.Value, nil); len(moreSensitivePaths) != 0 {\n738: \t\tret.Value = marks.MarkPaths(ret.Value, marks.Sensitive, moreSensitivePaths)\n739: \t}\n740: \n741: \treturn ret, deferred, diags\n742: }\n743: \n744: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) plan(\n745: \tctx EvalContext,\n746: \tplannedChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange,\n747: \tcurrentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject,\n748: \tcreateBeforeDestroy bool,\n749: \tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance,\n750: ) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n751: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n752: \tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\n753: \tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n754: \n755: \tresource := n.Addr.Resource.Resource\n756: \tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n757: \n758: \tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n759: \tif err != nil {\n760: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags.Append(err)\n761: \t}\n762: \n763: \tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(resource)\n764: \tif schema == nil {\n765: \t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n766: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", resource.Type))\n767: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n768: \t}\n769: \n770: \t// If we're importing and generating config, generate it now.\n771: \tif n.Config == nil {\n772: \t\t// This shouldn't happen. A node that isn't generating config should\n773: \t\t// have embedded config, and the rest of Terraform should enforce this.\n774: \t\t// If, however, we didn't do things correctly the next line will panic,\n775: \t\t// so let's not do that and return an error message with more context.\n776: \n777: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n778: \t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n779: \t\t\t\"Resource has no configuration\",\n780: \t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Terraform attempted to process a resource at %s that has no configuration. This is a bug in Terraform; please report it!\", n.Addr.String())))\n781: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n782: \t}\n783: \n784: \tconfig := *n.Config\n785: \n786: \tcheckRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error\n787: \tif n.preDestroyRefresh {\n788: \t\tcheckRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning\n789: \t}\n790: \n791: \tif plannedChange != nil {\n792: \t\t// If we already planned the action, we stick to that plan\n793: \t\tcreateBeforeDestroy = plannedChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete\n794: \t}\n795: \n796: \t// Evaluate the configuration\n797: \tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\n798: \n799: \tkeyData = EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\n800: \n801: \tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n802: \t\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\n803: \t\tn.Config.Preconditions,\n804: \t\tctx, n.Addr, keyData,\n805: \t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n806: \t)\n807: \tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n808: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n809: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags // failed preconditions prevent further evaluation\n810: \t}\n811: \n812: \t// If we have a previous plan and the action was a noop, then the only\n813: \t// reason we're in this method was to evaluate the preconditions. There's\n814: \t// no need to re-plan this resource.\n815: \tif plannedChange != nil && plannedChange.Action == plans.NoOp {\n816: \t\treturn plannedChange, currentState.DeepCopy(), deferred, keyData, diags\n817: \t}\n818: \n819: \torigConfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\n820: \tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\n821: \tdiags = diags.Append(\n822: \t\tvalidateResourceForbiddenEphemeralValues(ctx, origConfigVal, schema).InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String()),\n823: \t)\n824: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n825: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n826: \t}\n827: \n828: \tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\n829: \tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\n830: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n831: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n832: \t}\n833: \n834: \tvar priorVal cty.Value\n835: \tvar priorValTainted cty.Value\n836: \tvar priorPrivate []byte\n837: \tif currentState != nil {\n838: \t\tif currentState.Status != states.ObjectTainted {\n839: \t\t\tpriorVal = currentState.Value\n840: \t\t\tpriorPrivate = currentState.Private\n841: \t\t} else {\n842: \t\t\t// If the prior state is tainted then we'll proceed below like\n843: \t\t\t// we're creating an entirely new object, but then turn it into\n844: \t\t\t// a synthetic \"Replace\" change at the end, creating the same\n845: \t\t\t// result as if the provider had marked at least one argument\n846: \t\t\t// change as \"requires replacement\".\n847: \t\t\tpriorValTainted = currentState.Value\n848: \t\t\tpriorVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\n849: \t\t}\n850: \t} else {\n851: \t\tpriorVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\n852: \t}\n853: \n854: \tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] Re-validating config for %q\", n.Addr)\n855: \t// Allow the provider to validate the final set of values.  The config was\n856: \t// statically validated early on, but there may have been unknown values\n857: \t// which the provider could not validate at the time.\n858: \t//\n859: \t// TODO: It would be more correct to validate the config after\n860: \t// ignore_changes has been applied, but the current implementation cannot\n861: \t// exclude computed-only attributes when given the `all` option.\n862: \n863: \t// we must unmark and use the original config, since the ignore_changes\n864: \t// handling below needs access to the marks.\n865: \tunmarkedConfigVal, _ := origConfigVal.UnmarkDeep()\n866: \tvalidateResp := provider.ValidateResourceConfig(\n867: \t\tproviders.ValidateResourceConfigRequest{\n868: \t\t\tTypeName: n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n869: \t\t\tConfig:   unmarkedConfigVal,\n870: \t\t},\n871: \t)\n872: \tdiags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\n873: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n874: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n875: \t}\n876: \n877: \t// ignore_changes is meant to only apply to the configuration, so it must\n878: \t// be applied before we generate a plan. This ensures the config used for\n879: \t// the proposed value, the proposed value itself, and the config presented\n880: \t// to the provider in the PlanResourceChange request all agree on the\n881: \t// starting values.\n882: \t// Here we operate on the marked values, so as to revert any changes to the\n883: \t// marks as well as the value.\n884: \tconfigValIgnored, ignoreChangeDiags := n.processIgnoreChanges(priorVal, origConfigVal, schema)\n885: \tdiags = diags.Append(ignoreChangeDiags)\n886: \tif ignoreChangeDiags.HasErrors() {\n887: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n888: \t}\n889: \n890: \t// Create an unmarked version of our config val and our prior val.\n891: \t// Store the paths for the config val to re-mark after we've sent things\n892: \t// over the wire.\n893: \tunmarkedConfigVal, unmarkedPaths := configValIgnored.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n894: \tunmarkedPriorVal, _ := priorVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n895: \n896: \tproposedNewVal := objchange.ProposedNew(schema, unmarkedPriorVal, unmarkedConfigVal)\n897: \n898: \t// Call pre-diff hook\n899: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n900: \t\treturn h.PreDiff(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, priorVal, proposedNewVal)\n901: \t}))\n902: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n903: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n904: \t}\n905: \n906: \tvar resp providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse\n907: \tif n.override != nil {\n908: \t\t// Then we have an override to apply for this change. But, overrides\n909: \t\t// only matter when we are creating a resource for the first time as we\n910: \t\t// only apply computed values.\n911: \t\tif priorVal.IsNull() {\n912: \t\t\t// Then we are actually creating something, so let's populate the\n913: \t\t\t// computed values from our override value.\n914: \t\t\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.PlanComputedValuesForResource(proposedNewVal, schema)\n915: \t\t\tresp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{\n916: \t\t\t\tPlannedState: override,\n917: \t\t\t\tDiagnostics:  overrideDiags,\n918: \t\t\t}\n919: \t\t} else {\n920: \t\t\t// This is an update operation, and we don't actually have any\n921: \t\t\t// computed values that need to be applied.\n922: \t\t\tresp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{\n923: \t\t\t\tPlannedState: proposedNewVal,\n924: \t\t\t}\n925: \t\t}\n926: \t} else {\n927: \t\tresp = provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{\n928: \t\t\tTypeName:         n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n929: \t\t\tConfig:           unmarkedConfigVal,\n930: \t\t\tPriorState:       unmarkedPriorVal,\n931: \t\t\tProposedNewState: proposedNewVal,\n932: \t\t\tPriorPrivate:     priorPrivate,\n933: \t\t\tProviderMeta:     metaConfigVal,\n934: \t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n935: \t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n936: \t\t\t},\n937: \t\t})\n938: \t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n939: \t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n940: \t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n941: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n942: \t\t}\n943: \t}\n944: \tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\n945: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n946: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n947: \t}\n948: \n949: \t// We mark this node as deferred at a later point when we know the complete change\n950: \tif resp.Deferred != nil {\n951: \t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n952: \t}\n953: \n954: \tplannedNewVal := resp.PlannedState\n955: \tplannedPrivate := resp.PlannedPrivate\n956: \n957: \t// These checks are only relevant if the provider is not deferring the\n958: \t// change.\n959: \tif deferred == nil {\n960: \t\tif plannedNewVal == cty.NilVal {\n961: \t\t\t// Should never happen. Since real-world providers return via RPC a nil\n962: \t\t\t// is always a bug in the client-side stub. This is more likely caused\n963: \t\t\t// by an incompletely-configured mock provider in tests, though.\n964: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"PlanResourceChange of %s produced nil value\", n.Addr))\n965: \t\t}\n966: \n967: \t\t// We allow the planned new value to disagree with configuration _values_\n968: \t\t// here, since that allows the provider to do special logic like a\n969: \t\t// DiffSuppressFunc, but we still require that the provider produces\n970: \t\t// a value whose type conforms to the schema.\n971: \t\tfor _, err := range plannedNewVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\n972: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n973: \t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n974: \t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n975: \t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n976: \t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n977: \t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.Addr.String()),\n978: \t\t\t\t),\n979: \t\t\t))\n980: \t\t}\n981: \n982: \t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n983: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n984: \t\t}\n985: \n986: \t\tif errs := objchange.AssertPlanValid(schema, unmarkedPriorVal, unmarkedConfigVal, plannedNewVal); len(errs) > 0 {\n987: \t\t\tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\n988: \t\t\t\t// The shimming of the old type system in the legacy SDK is not precise\n989: \t\t\t\t// enough to pass this consistency check, so we'll give it a pass here,\n990: \t\t\t\t// but we will generate a warning about it so that we are more likely\n991: \t\t\t\t// to notice in the logs if an inconsistency beyond the type system\n992: \t\t\t\t// leads to a downstream provider failure.\n993: \t\t\t\tvar buf strings.Builder\n994: \t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf,\n995: \t\t\t\t\t\"[WARN] Provider %q produced an invalid plan for %s, but we are tolerating it because it is using the legacy plugin SDK.\\n    The following problems may be the cause of any confusing errors from downstream operations:\",\n996: \t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr,\n997: \t\t\t\t)\n998: \t\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n999: \t\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"\\n      - %s\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\n1000: \t\t\t\t}\n1001: \t\t\t\tlog.Print(buf.String())\n1002: \t\t\t} else {\n1003: \t\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n1004: \t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n1005: \t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n1006: \t\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n1007: \t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n1008: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n1009: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.Addr.String()),\n1010: \t\t\t\t\t\t),\n1011: \t\t\t\t\t))\n1012: \t\t\t\t}\n1013: \t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1014: \t\t\t}\n1015: \t\t}\n1016: \t}\n1017: \n1018: \tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\n1019: \t\t// Because we allow legacy providers to depart from the contract and\n1020: \t\t// return changes to non-computed values, the plan response may have\n1021: \t\t// altered values that were already suppressed with ignore_changes.\n1022: \t\t// A prime example of this is where providers attempt to obfuscate\n1023: \t\t// config data by turning the config value into a hash and storing the\n1024: \t\t// hash value in the state. There are enough cases of this in existing\n1025: \t\t// providers that we must accommodate the behavior for now, so for\n1026: \t\t// ignore_changes to work at all on these values, we will revert the\n1027: \t\t// ignored values once more.\n1028: \t\t// A nil schema is passed to processIgnoreChanges to indicate that we\n1029: \t\t// don't want to fixup a config value according to the schema when\n1030: \t\t// ignoring \"all\", rather we are reverting provider imposed changes.\n1031: \t\tplannedNewVal, ignoreChangeDiags = n.processIgnoreChanges(unmarkedPriorVal, plannedNewVal, nil)\n1032: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(ignoreChangeDiags)\n1033: \t\tif ignoreChangeDiags.HasErrors() {\n1034: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1035: \t\t}\n1036: \t}\n1037: \n1038: \t// Add the marks back to the planned new value -- this must happen after\n1039: \t// ignore changes have been processed. We add in the schema marks as well,\n1040: \t// to ensure that provider defined private attributes are marked correctly\n1041: \t// here.\n1042: \tunmarkedPlannedNewVal := plannedNewVal\n1043: \tplannedNewVal = plannedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\n1044: \tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(plannedNewVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\n1045: \t\tplannedNewVal = marks.MarkPaths(plannedNewVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\n1046: \t}\n1047: \n1048: \treqRep, reqRepDiags := getRequiredReplaces(unmarkedPriorVal, unmarkedPlannedNewVal, resp.RequiresReplace, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr)\n1049: \tdiags = diags.Append(reqRepDiags)\n1050: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1051: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1052: \t}\n1053: \n1054: \taction, actionReason := getAction(n.Addr, unmarkedPriorVal, unmarkedPlannedNewVal, createBeforeDestroy, forceReplace, reqRep)\n1055: \n1056: \tif action.IsReplace() {\n1057: \t\t// In this strange situation we want to produce a change object that\n1058: \t\t// shows our real prior object but has a _new_ object that is built\n1059: \t\t// from a null prior object, since we're going to delete the one\n1060: \t\t// that has all the computed values on it.\n1061: \t\t//\n1062: \t\t// Therefore we'll ask the provider to plan again here, giving it\n1063: \t\t// a null object for the prior, and then we'll meld that with the\n1064: \t\t// _actual_ prior state to produce a correctly-shaped replace change.\n1065: \t\t// The resulting change should show any computed attributes changing\n1066: \t\t// from known prior values to unknown values, unless the provider is\n1067: \t\t// able to predict new values for any of these computed attributes.\n1068: \t\tnullPriorVal := cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\n1069: \n1070: \t\t// Since there is no prior state to compare after replacement, we need\n1071: \t\t// a new unmarked config from our original with no ignored values.\n1072: \t\tunmarkedConfigVal := origConfigVal\n1073: \t\tif origConfigVal.ContainsMarked() {\n1074: \t\t\tunmarkedConfigVal, _ = origConfigVal.UnmarkDeep()\n1075: \t\t}\n1076: \n1077: \t\t// create a new proposed value from the null state and the config\n1078: \t\tproposedNewVal = objchange.ProposedNew(schema, nullPriorVal, unmarkedConfigVal)\n1079: \n1080: \t\tif n.override != nil {\n1081: \t\t\t// In this case, we are always creating the resource so we don't\n1082: \t\t\t// do any validation, and just call out to the mocking library.\n1083: \t\t\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.PlanComputedValuesForResource(proposedNewVal, schema)\n1084: \t\t\tresp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{\n1085: \t\t\t\tPlannedState: override,\n1086: \t\t\t\tDiagnostics:  overrideDiags,\n1087: \t\t\t}\n1088: \t\t} else {\n1089: \t\t\tresp = provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{\n1090: \t\t\t\tTypeName:         n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n1091: \t\t\t\tConfig:           unmarkedConfigVal,\n1092: \t\t\t\tPriorState:       nullPriorVal,\n1093: \t\t\t\tProposedNewState: proposedNewVal,\n1094: \t\t\t\tPriorPrivate:     plannedPrivate,\n1095: \t\t\t\tProviderMeta:     metaConfigVal,\n1096: \t\t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n1097: \t\t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n1098: \t\t\t\t},\n1099: \t\t\t})\n1100: \n1101: \t\t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n1102: \t\t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n1103: \t\t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n1104: \t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n1105: \t\t\t}\n1106: \t\t}\n1107: \t\t// We need to tread carefully here, since if there are any warnings\n1108: \t\t// in here they probably also came out of our previous call to\n1109: \t\t// PlanResourceChange above, and so we don't want to repeat them.\n1110: \t\t// Consequently, we break from the usual pattern here and only\n1111: \t\t// append these new diagnostics if there's at least one error inside.\n1112: \t\tif resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n1113: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\n1114: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1115: \t\t}\n1116: \n1117: \t\tif deferred == nil && resp.Deferred != nil {\n1118: \t\t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n1119: \t\t}\n1120: \n1121: \t\tplannedNewVal = resp.PlannedState\n1122: \t\tplannedPrivate = resp.PlannedPrivate\n1123: \n1124: \t\tif len(unmarkedPaths) > 0 {\n1125: \t\t\tplannedNewVal = plannedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\n1126: \t\t}\n1127: \n1128: \t\tfor _, err := range plannedNewVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\n1129: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n1130: \t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n1131: \t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n1132: \t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n1133: \t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s%s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n1134: \t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr, tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n1135: \t\t\t\t),\n1136: \t\t\t))\n1137: \t\t}\n1138: \t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1139: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1140: \t\t}\n1141: \t}\n1142: \n1143: \t// If our prior value was tainted then we actually want this to appear\n1144: \t// as a replace change, even though so far we've been treating it as a\n1145: \t// create.\n1146: \tif action == plans.Create && !priorValTainted.IsNull() {\n1147: \t\tif createBeforeDestroy {\n1148: \t\t\taction = plans.CreateThenDelete\n1149: \t\t} else {\n1150: \t\t\taction = plans.DeleteThenCreate\n1151: \t\t}\n1152: \t\tpriorVal = priorValTainted\n1153: \t\tactionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceBecauseTainted\n1154: \t}\n1155: \n1156: \t// If we plan to change the sensitivity on some portion of the value, this\n1157: \t// is an Update action even when the values are otherwise equal.\n1158: \t//\n1159: \t// The marks should be normalized by being applied to the new value. We\n1160: \t// don't try to compare the marks we had collected from the config and\n1161: \t// schema, because the act of applying marks to a value may result in\n1162: \t// slightly different marks. For example marks within a set transfer to the\n1163: \t// entire set, and are not saved on the individual elements.\n1164: \tif action == plans.NoOp && !valueMarksEqual(plannedNewVal, priorVal) {\n1165: \t\taction = plans.Update\n1166: \t}\n1167: \n1168: \t// As a special case, if we have a previous diff (presumably from the plan\n1169: \t// phases, whereas we're now in the apply phase) and it was for a replace,\n1170: \t// we've already deleted the original object from state by the time we\n1171: \t// get here and so we would've ended up with a _create_ action this time,\n1172: \t// which we now need to paper over to get a result consistent with what\n1173: \t// we originally intended.\n1174: \tif plannedChange != nil {\n1175: \t\tprevChange := *plannedChange\n1176: \t\tif prevChange.Action.IsReplace() && action == plans.Create {\n1177: \t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] plan: %s treating Create change as %s change to match with earlier plan\", n.Addr, prevChange.Action)\n1178: \t\t\taction = prevChange.Action\n1179: \t\t\tpriorVal = prevChange.Before\n1180: \t\t}\n1181: \t}\n1182: \n1183: \t// Call post-refresh hook\n1184: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n1185: \t\treturn h.PostDiff(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, action, priorVal, plannedNewVal)\n1186: \t}))\n1187: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1188: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1189: \t}\n1190: \n1191: \t// Update our return plan\n1192: \tplan := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n1193: \t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n1194: \t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n1195: \t\tPrivate:      plannedPrivate,\n1196: \t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n1197: \t\tChange: plans.Change{\n1198: \t\t\tAction: action,\n1199: \t\t\tBefore: priorVal,\n1200: \t\t\t// Pass the marked planned value through in our change\n1201: \t\t\t// to propogate through evaluation.\n1202: \t\t\t// Marks will be removed when encoding.\n1203: \t\t\tAfter:           plannedNewVal,\n1204: \t\t\tGeneratedConfig: n.generatedConfigHCL,\n1205: \t\t},\n1206: \t\tActionReason:    actionReason,\n1207: \t\tRequiredReplace: reqRep,\n1208: \t}\n1209: \n1210: \t// Update our return state\n1211: \tstate := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n1212: \t\t// We use the special \"planned\" status here to note that this\n1213: \t\t// object's value is not yet complete. Objects with this status\n1214: \t\t// cannot be used during expression evaluation, so the caller\n1215: \t\t// must _also_ record the returned change in the active plan,\n1216: \t\t// which the expression evaluator will use in preference to this\n1217: \t\t// incomplete value recorded in the state.\n1218: \t\tStatus:  states.ObjectPlanned,\n1219: \t\tValue:   plannedNewVal,\n1220: \t\tPrivate: plannedPrivate,\n1221: \t}\n1222: \n1223: \treturn plan, state, deferred, keyData, diags\n1224: }\n1225: \n1226: func (n *NodeAbstractResource) processIgnoreChanges(prior, config cty.Value, schema *configschema.Block) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n1227: \t// ignore_changes only applies when an object already exists, since we\n1228: \t// can't ignore changes to a thing we've not created yet.\n1229: \tif prior.IsNull() {\n1230: \t\treturn config, nil\n1231: \t}\n1232: \n1233: \tignoreChanges := traversalsToPaths(n.Config.Managed.IgnoreChanges)\n1234: \tignoreAll := n.Config.Managed.IgnoreAllChanges\n1235: \n1236: \tif len(ignoreChanges) == 0 && !ignoreAll {\n1237: \t\treturn config, nil\n1238: \t}\n1239: \n1240: \tif ignoreAll {\n1241: \t\t// Legacy providers need up to clean up their invalid plans and ensure\n1242: \t\t// no changes are passed though, but that also means making an invalid\n1243: \t\t// config with computed values. In that case we just don't supply a\n1244: \t\t// schema and return the prior val directly.\n1245: \t\tif schema == nil {\n1246: \t\t\treturn prior, nil\n1247: \t\t}\n1248: \n1249: \t\t// If we are trying to ignore all attribute changes, we must filter\n1250: \t\t// computed attributes out from the prior state to avoid sending them\n1251: \t\t// to the provider as if they were included in the configuration.\n1252: \t\tret, _ := cty.Transform(prior, func(path cty.Path, v cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\n1253: \t\t\tattr := schema.AttributeByPath(path)\n1254: \t\t\tif attr != nil && attr.Computed && !attr.Optional {\n1255: \t\t\t\treturn cty.NullVal(v.Type()), nil\n1256: \t\t\t}\n1257: \n1258: \t\t\treturn v, nil\n1259: \t\t})\n1260: \n1261: \t\treturn ret, nil\n1262: \t}\n1263: \n1264: \tif prior.IsNull() || config.IsNull() {\n1265: \t\t// Ignore changes doesn't apply when we're creating for the first time.\n1266: \t\t// Proposed should never be null here, but if it is then we'll just let it be.\n1267: \t\treturn config, nil\n1268: \t}\n1269: \n1270: \tret, diags := processIgnoreChangesIndividual(prior, config, ignoreChanges)\n1271: \n1272: \treturn ret, diags\n1273: }\n1274: \n1275: // Convert the hcl.Traversal values we get form the configuration to the\n1276: // cty.Path values we need to operate on the cty.Values\n1277: func traversalsToPaths(traversals []hcl.Traversal) []cty.Path {\n1278: \tpaths := make([]cty.Path, len(traversals))\n1279: \tfor i, traversal := range traversals {\n1280: \t\tpath := traversalToPath(traversal)\n1281: \t\tpaths[i] = path\n1282: \t}\n1283: \treturn paths\n1284: }\n1285: \n1286: func traversalToPath(traversal hcl.Traversal) cty.Path {\n1287: \tpath := make(cty.Path, len(traversal))\n1288: \tfor si, step := range traversal {\n1289: \t\tswitch ts := step.(type) {\n1290: \t\tcase hcl.TraverseRoot:\n1291: \t\t\tpath[si] = cty.GetAttrStep{\n1292: \t\t\t\tName: ts.Name,\n1293: \t\t\t}\n1294: \t\tcase hcl.TraverseAttr:\n1295: \t\t\tpath[si] = cty.GetAttrStep{\n1296: \t\t\t\tName: ts.Name,\n1297: \t\t\t}\n1298: \t\tcase hcl.TraverseIndex:\n1299: \t\t\tpath[si] = cty.IndexStep{\n1300: \t\t\t\tKey: ts.Key,\n1301: \t\t\t}\n1302: \t\tdefault:\n1303: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unsupported traversal step %#v\", step))\n1304: \t\t}\n1305: \t}\n1306: \treturn path\n1307: }\n1308: \n1309: func processIgnoreChangesIndividual(prior, config cty.Value, ignoreChangesPath []cty.Path) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n1310: \ttype ignoreChange struct {\n1311: \t\t// Path is the full path, minus any trailing map index\n1312: \t\tpath cty.Path\n1313: \t\t// Value is the value we are to retain at the above path. If there is a\n1314: \t\t// key value, this must be a map and the desired value will be at the\n1315: \t\t// key index.\n1316: \t\tvalue cty.Value\n1317: \t\t// Key is the index key if the ignored path ends in a map index.\n1318: \t\tkey cty.Value\n1319: \t}\n1320: \tvar ignoredValues []ignoreChange\n1321: \n1322: \t// Find the actual changes first and store them in the ignoreChange struct.\n1323: \t// If the change was to a map value, and the key doesn't exist in the\n1324: \t// config, it would never be visited in the transform walk.\n1325: \tfor _, icPath := range ignoreChangesPath {\n1326: \t\tkey := cty.NullVal(cty.String)\n1327: \t\t// check for a map index, since maps are the only structure where we\n1328: \t\t// could have invalid path steps.\n1329: \t\tlast, ok := icPath[len(icPath)-1].(cty.IndexStep)\n1330: \t\tif ok {\n1331: \t\t\tif last.Key.Type() == cty.String {\n1332: \t\t\t\ticPath = icPath[:len(icPath)-1]\n1333: \t\t\t\tkey = last.Key\n1334: \t\t\t}\n1335: \t\t}\n1336: \n1337: \t\t// The structure should have been validated already, and we already\n1338: \t\t// trimmed the trailing map index. Any other intermediate index error\n1339: \t\t// means we wouldn't be able to apply the value below, so no need to\n1340: \t\t// record this.\n1341: \t\tp, err := icPath.Apply(prior)\n1342: \t\tif err != nil {\n1343: \t\t\tcontinue\n1344: \t\t}\n1345: \t\tc, err := icPath.Apply(config)\n1346: \t\tif err != nil {\n1347: \t\t\tcontinue\n1348: \t\t}\n1349: \n1350: \t\t// If this is a map, it is checking the entire map value for equality\n1351: \t\t// rather than the individual key. This means that the change is stored\n1352: \t\t// here even if our ignored key doesn't change. That is OK since it\n1353: \t\t// won't cause any changes in the transformation, but allows us to skip\n1354: \t\t// breaking up the maps and checking for key existence here too.\n1355: \t\tif !p.RawEquals(c) {\n1356: \t\t\t// there a change to ignore at this path, store the prior value\n1357: \t\t\tignoredValues = append(ignoredValues, ignoreChange{icPath, p, key})\n1358: \t\t}\n1359: \t}\n1360: \n1361: \tif len(ignoredValues) == 0 {\n1362: \t\treturn config, nil\n1363: \t}\n1364: \n1365: \tret, _ := cty.Transform(config, func(path cty.Path, v cty.Value) (cty.Value, error) {\n1366: \t\t// Easy path for when we are only matching the entire value. The only\n1367: \t\t// values we break up for inspection are maps.\n1368: \t\tif !v.Type().IsMapType() {\n1369: \t\t\tfor _, ignored := range ignoredValues {\n1370: \t\t\t\tif path.Equals(ignored.path) {\n1371: \t\t\t\t\treturn ignored.value, nil\n1372: \t\t\t\t}\n1373: \t\t\t}\n1374: \t\t\treturn v, nil\n1375: \t\t}\n1376: \t\t// We now know this must be a map, so we need to accumulate the values\n1377: \t\t// key-by-key.\n1378: \n1379: \t\tif !v.IsNull() && !v.IsKnown() {\n1380: \t\t\t// since v is not known, we cannot ignore individual keys\n1381: \t\t\treturn v, nil\n1382: \t\t}\n1383: \n1384: \t\t// The map values will remain as cty values, so we only need to store\n1385: \t\t// the marks from the outer map itself\n1386: \t\tv, vMarks := v.Unmark()\n1387: \n1388: \t\t// The configMap is the current configuration value, which we will\n1389: \t\t// mutate based on the ignored paths and the prior map value.\n1390: \t\tvar configMap map[string]cty.Value\n1391: \t\tswitch {\n1392: \t\tcase v.IsNull() || v.LengthInt() == 0:\n1393: \t\t\tconfigMap = map[string]cty.Value{}\n1394: \t\tdefault:\n1395: \t\t\tconfigMap = v.AsValueMap()\n1396: \t\t}\n1397: \n1398: \t\tfor _, ignored := range ignoredValues {\n1399: \t\t\tif !path.Equals(ignored.path) {\n1400: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1401: \t\t\t}\n1402: \n1403: \t\t\tif ignored.key.IsNull() {\n1404: \t\t\t\t// The map address is confirmed to match at this point,\n1405: \t\t\t\t// so if there is no key, we want the entire map and can\n1406: \t\t\t\t// stop accumulating values.\n1407: \t\t\t\treturn ignored.value, nil\n1408: \t\t\t}\n1409: \t\t\t// Now we know we are ignoring a specific index of this map, so get\n1410: \t\t\t// the config map and modify, add, or remove the desired key.\n1411: \n1412: \t\t\t// We also need to create a prior map, so we can check for\n1413: \t\t\t// existence while getting the value, because Value.Index will\n1414: \t\t\t// return null for a key with a null value and for a non-existent\n1415: \t\t\t// key.\n1416: \t\t\tvar priorMap map[string]cty.Value\n1417: \n1418: \t\t\t// We need to drop the marks from the ignored map for handling. We\n1419: \t\t\t// don't need to store these, as we now know the ignored value is\n1420: \t\t\t// only within the map, not the map itself.\n1421: \t\t\tignoredVal, _ := ignored.value.Unmark()\n1422: \n1423: \t\t\tswitch {\n1424: \t\t\tcase ignored.value.IsNull() || ignoredVal.LengthInt() == 0:\n1425: \t\t\t\tpriorMap = map[string]cty.Value{}\n1426: \t\t\tdefault:\n1427: \t\t\t\tpriorMap = ignoredVal.AsValueMap()\n1428: \t\t\t}\n1429: \n1430: \t\t\tkey := ignored.key.AsString()\n1431: \t\t\tpriorElem, keep := priorMap[key]\n1432: \n1433: \t\t\tswitch {\n1434: \t\t\tcase !keep:\n1435: \t\t\t\t// this didn't exist in the old map value, so we're keeping the\n1436: \t\t\t\t// \"absence\" of the key by removing it from the config\n1437: \t\t\t\tdelete(configMap, key)\n1438: \t\t\tdefault:\n1439: \t\t\t\tconfigMap[key] = priorElem\n1440: \t\t\t}\n1441: \t\t}\n1442: \n1443: \t\tvar newVal cty.Value\n1444: \t\tswitch {\n1445: \t\tcase len(configMap) > 0:\n1446: \t\t\tnewVal = cty.MapVal(configMap)\n1447: \t\tcase v.IsNull():\n1448: \t\t\t// if the config value was null, and no values remain in the map,\n1449: \t\t\t// reset the value to null.\n1450: \t\t\tnewVal = v\n1451: \t\tdefault:\n1452: \t\t\tnewVal = cty.MapValEmpty(v.Type().ElementType())\n1453: \t\t}\n1454: \n1455: \t\tif len(vMarks) > 0 {\n1456: \t\t\tnewVal = newVal.WithMarks(vMarks)\n1457: \t\t}\n1458: \n1459: \t\treturn newVal, nil\n1460: \t})\n1461: \treturn ret, nil\n1462: }\n1463: \n1464: // readDataSource handles everything needed to call ReadDataSource on the provider.\n1465: // A previously evaluated configVal can be passed in, or a new one is generated\n1466: // from the resource configuration.\n1467: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) readDataSource(ctx EvalContext, configVal cty.Value) (cty.Value, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n1468: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n1469: \tvar newVal cty.Value\n1470: \tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n1471: \n1472: \tconfig := *n.Config\n1473: \tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n1474: \n1475: \tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n1476: \tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n1477: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1478: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1479: \t}\n1480: \tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\n1481: \tif schema == nil {\n1482: \t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n1483: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider %q does not support data source %q\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\n1484: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1485: \t}\n1486: \n1487: \tmetaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)\n1488: \tdiags = diags.Append(metaDiags)\n1489: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1490: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1491: \t}\n1492: \n1493: \t// Unmark before sending to provider, will re-mark before returning\n1494: \tvar pvm []cty.PathValueMarks\n1495: \tconfigVal, pvm = configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n1496: \n1497: \tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readDataSource: Re-validating config for %s\", n.Addr)\n1498: \tvalidateResp := provider.ValidateDataResourceConfig(\n1499: \t\tproviders.ValidateDataResourceConfigRequest{\n1500: \t\t\tTypeName: n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type,\n1501: \t\t\tConfig:   configVal,\n1502: \t\t},\n1503: \t)\n1504: \tdiags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\n1505: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1506: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1507: \t}\n1508: \n1509: \t// If we get down here then our configuration is complete and we're read\n1510: \t// to actually call the provider to read the data.\n1511: \tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readDataSource: %s configuration is complete, so reading from provider\", n.Addr)\n1512: \n1513: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n1514: \t\treturn h.PreApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, plans.Read, cty.NullVal(configVal.Type()), configVal)\n1515: \t}))\n1516: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1517: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1518: \t}\n1519: \n1520: \tvar resp providers.ReadDataSourceResponse\n1521: \tif n.override != nil {\n1522: \t\toverride, overrideDiags := mocking.ComputedValuesForDataSource(configVal, mocking.MockedData{\n1523: \t\t\tValue: n.override.Values,\n1524: \t\t\tRange: n.override.ValuesRange,\n1525: \t\t}, schema)\n1526: \t\tresp = providers.ReadDataSourceResponse{\n1527: \t\t\tState:       override,\n1528: \t\t\tDiagnostics: overrideDiags,\n1529: \t\t}\n1530: \t} else {\n1531: \t\tresp = provider.ReadDataSource(providers.ReadDataSourceRequest{\n1532: \t\t\tTypeName:     n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type,\n1533: \t\t\tConfig:       configVal,\n1534: \t\t\tProviderMeta: metaConfigVal,\n1535: \t\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n1536: \t\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,\n1537: \t\t\t},\n1538: \t\t})\n1539: \n1540: \t\t// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not\n1541: \t\t// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.\n1542: \t\tif resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {\n1543: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))\n1544: \t\t}\n1545: \n1546: \t\tif resp.Deferred != nil {\n1547: \t\t\tdeferred = resp.Deferred\n1548: \t\t}\n1549: \t}\n1550: \tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))\n1551: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1552: \t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1553: \t}\n1554: \tnewVal = resp.State\n1555: \tif newVal == cty.NilVal {\n1556: \t\t// This can happen with incompletely-configured mocks. We'll allow it\n1557: \t\t// and treat it as an alias for a properly-typed null value.\n1558: \t\tnewVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())\n1559: \t}\n1560: \n1561: \t// We don't want to run the checks if the data source read is deferred\n1562: \tif deferred == nil {\n1563: \t\tfor _, err := range newVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\n1564: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n1565: \t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n1566: \t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid object\",\n1567: \t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n1568: \t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q produced an invalid value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n1569: \t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider, tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.Addr.String()),\n1570: \t\t\t\t),\n1571: \t\t\t))\n1572: \t\t}\n1573: \t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1574: \t\t\treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1575: \t\t}\n1576: \n1577: \t\tif newVal.IsNull() {\n1578: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n1579: \t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n1580: \t\t\t\t\"Provider produced null object\",\n1581: \t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n1582: \t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q produced a null value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n1583: \t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr,\n1584: \t\t\t\t),\n1585: \t\t\t))\n1586: \t\t}\n1587: \n1588: \t\tif !newVal.IsNull() && !newVal.IsWhollyKnown() {\n1589: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n1590: \t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n1591: \t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid object\",\n1592: \t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n1593: \t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q produced a value for %s that is not wholly known.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n1594: \t\t\t\t\tn.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr,\n1595: \t\t\t\t),\n1596: \t\t\t))\n1597: \n1598: \t\t\t// We'll still save the object, but we need to eliminate any unknown\n1599: \t\t\t// values first because we can't serialize them in the state file.\n1600: \t\t\t// Note that this may cause set elements to be coalesced if they\n1601: \t\t\t// differed only by having unknown values, but we don't worry about\n1602: \t\t\t// that here because we're saving the value only for inspection\n1603: \t\t\t// purposes; the error we added above will halt the graph walk.\n1604: \t\t\tnewVal = cty.UnknownAsNull(newVal)\n1605: \t\t}\n1606: \t}\n1607: \tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(pvm)\n1608: \tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\n1609: \t\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\n1610: \t}\n1611: \n1612: \tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n1613: \t\treturn h.PostApply(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, newVal, diags.Err())\n1614: \t}))\n1615: \n1616: \treturn newVal, deferred, diags\n1617: }\n1618: \n1619: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) providerMetas(ctx EvalContext) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n1620: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n1621: \tmetaConfigVal := cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\n1622: \n1623: \t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n1624: \tif err != nil {\n1625: \t\treturn metaConfigVal, diags.Append(err)\n1626: \t}\n1627: \tif n.ProviderMetas != nil {\n1628: \t\tif m, ok := n.ProviderMetas[n.ResolvedProvider.Provider]; ok && m != nil {\n1629: \t\t\t// if the provider doesn't support this feature, throw an error\n1630: \t\t\tif providerSchema.ProviderMeta.Block == nil {\n1631: \t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n1632: \t\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n1633: \t\t\t\t\tSummary:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Provider %s doesn't support provider_meta\", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String()),\n1634: \t\t\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"The resource %s belongs to a provider that doesn't support provider_meta blocks\", n.Addr.Resource),\n1635: \t\t\t\t\tSubject:  &m.ProviderRange,\n1636: \t\t\t\t})\n1637: \t\t\t} else {\n1638: \t\t\t\tvar configDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n1639: \t\t\t\tmetaConfigVal, _, configDiags = ctx.EvaluateBlock(m.Config, providerSchema.ProviderMeta.Block, nil, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey)\n1640: \t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\n1641: \t\t\t}\n1642: \t\t}\n1643: \t}\n1644: \treturn metaConfigVal, diags\n1645: }\n1646: \n1647: // planDataSource deals with the main part of the data resource lifecycle:\n1648: // either actually reading from the data source or generating a plan to do so.\n1649: //\n1650: // currentState is the current state for the data source, and the new state is\n1651: // returned. While data sources are read-only, we need to start with the prior\n1652: // state to determine if we have a change or not.  If we needed to read a new\n1653: // value, but it still matches the previous state, then we can record a NoNop\n1654: // change. If the states don't match then we record a Read change so that the\n1655: // new value is applied to the state.\n1656: //\n1657: // The cases where a data source will generate a planned change instead\n1658: // of finishing during the plan are:\n1659: //\n1660: //   - Its config has unknown values or it depends on a resource with pending changes.\n1661: //     (Note that every data source that is DeferredPrereq should also fit this description.)\n1662: //   - We attempted a read request, but the provider says we're deferred.\n1663: //   - It's nested in a check block, and should always read again during apply.\n1664: func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) planDataSource(ctx EvalContext, checkRuleSeverity tfdiags.Severity, skipPlanChanges, dependencyDeferred bool) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n1665: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n1666: \tvar keyData instances.RepetitionData\n1667: \tvar configVal cty.Value\n1668: \n1669: \tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n1670: \tif dependencyDeferred {\n1671: \t\t// If a dependency of this data source was deferred, then we're going\n1672: \t\t// to end up deferring this whatever happens. So, our default status\n1673: \t\t// is deferred. If the provider indicates this resource should be\n1674: \t\t// deferred for another reason, that reason should take priority over\n1675: \t\t// this one.\n1676: \t\tdeferred = &providers.Deferred{\n1677: \t\t\tReason: providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq,\n1678: \t\t}\n1679: \t}\n1680: \n1681: \t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n1682: \tif err != nil {\n1683: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags.Append(err)\n1684: \t}\n1685: \n1686: \tconfig := *n.Config\n1687: \tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource)\n1688: \tif schema == nil {\n1689: \t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n1690: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider %q does not support data source %q\", n.ResolvedProvider, n.Addr.ContainingResource().Resource.Type))\n1691: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1692: \t}\n1693: \n1694: \tobjTy := schema.ImpliedType()\n1695: \tpriorVal := cty.NullVal(objTy)\n1696: \n1697: \tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(config.ForEach, ctx, false)\n1698: \tkeyData = EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\n1699: \n1700: \tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n1701: \t\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\n1702: \t\tn.Config.Preconditions,\n1703: \t\tctx, n.Addr, keyData,\n1704: \t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n1705: \t)\n1706: \tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n1707: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1708: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags // failed preconditions prevent further evaluation\n1709: \t}\n1710: \n1711: \tvar configDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n1712: \tconfigVal, _, configDiags = ctx.EvaluateBlock(config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\n1713: \tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\n1714: \tdiags = diags.Append(\n1715: \t\tvalidateResourceForbiddenEphemeralValues(ctx, configVal, schema).InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String()),\n1716: \t)\n1717: \tif diags.HasErrors() {\n1718: \t\treturn nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags\n1719: \t}\n1720: \tunmarkedConfigVal, unmarkedPaths := configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n1721: \n1722: \tcheck, nested := n.nestedInCheckBlock()\n1723: \tif nested {\n1724: \t\t// Going forward from this point, the only reason we will fail is\n1725: \t\t// that the data source fails to load its data. Normally, this would\n1726: \t\t// cancel the entire plan and this error message would bubble its way\n1727: \t\t// back up to the user.\n1728: \t\t//\n1729: \t\t// But, if we are in a check block then we don't want this data block to\n1730: \t\t// cause the plan to fail. We also need to report a status on the data\n1731: \t\t// block so the check processing later on knows whether to attempt to\n1732: \t\t// process the checks. Either we'll report the data block as failed\n1733: \t\t// if/when we load the data block later, or we want to report it as a\n1734: \t\t// success overall.\n1735: \t\t//\n1736: \t\t// Therefore, we create a deferred function here that will check if the\n1737: \t\t// status for the check has been updated yet, and if not we will set it\n1738: \t\t// to be StatusPass. The rest of this function will only update the\n1739: \t\t// status if it should be StatusFail.\n1740: \t\tdefer func() {\n1741: \t\t\tstatus := ctx.Checks().ObjectCheckStatus(check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module))\n1742: \t\t\tif status == checks.StatusUnknown {\n1743: \t\t\t\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckResult(check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module), addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, checks.StatusPass)\n1744: \t\t\t}\n1745: \t\t}()\n1746: \t}\n1747: \n1748: \tconfigKnown := configVal.IsWhollyKnown()\n1749: \tdepsPending := n.dependenciesHavePendingChanges(ctx)\n1750: \t// If our configuration contains any unknown values, or we depend on any\n1751: \t// unknown values then we must defer the read to the apply phase by\n1752: \t// producing a \"Read\" change for this resource, and a placeholder value for\n1753: \t// it in the state.\n1754: \tif depsPending || !configKnown {\n1755: \t\t// We can't plan any changes if we're only refreshing, so the only\n1756: \t\t// value we can set here is whatever was in state previously.\n1757: \t\tif skipPlanChanges {\n1758: \t\t\tplannedNewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n1759: \t\t\t\tValue:  priorVal,\n1760: \t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n1761: \t\t\t}\n1762: \n1763: \t\t\treturn nil, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\n1764: \t\t}\n1765: \n1766: \t\tvar reason plans.ResourceInstanceChangeActionReason\n1767: \t\tswitch {\n1768: \t\tcase !configKnown:\n1769: \t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] planDataSource: %s configuration not fully known yet, so deferring to apply phase\", n.Addr)\n1770: \t\t\treason = plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseConfigUnknown\n1771: \t\tcase depsPending:\n1772: \t\t\t// NOTE: depsPending can be true at the same time as configKnown\n1773: \t\t\t// is false; configKnown takes precedence because it's more\n1774: \t\t\t// specific.\n1775: \t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] planDataSource: %s configuration is fully known, at least one dependency has changes pending\", n.Addr)\n1776: \t\t\treason = plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseDependencyPending\n1777: \t\t}\n1778: \n1779: \t\tproposedNewVal := objchange.PlannedDataResourceObject(schema, unmarkedConfigVal)\n1780: \n1781: \t\t// even though we are only returning the config value because we can't\n1782: \t\t// yet read the data source, we need to incorporate the schema marks so\n1783: \t\t// that downstream consumers can detect them when planning.\n1784: \t\tproposedNewVal = proposedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\n1785: \t\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(proposedNewVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\n1786: \t\t\tproposedNewVal = marks.MarkPaths(proposedNewVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\n1787: \t\t}\n1788: \n1789: \t\t// Apply detects that the data source will need to be read by the After\n1790: \t\t// value containing unknowns from PlanDataResourceObject.\n1791: \t\tplannedChange := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n1792: \t\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n1793: \t\t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n1794: \t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n1795: \t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n1796: \t\t\t\tAction: plans.Read,\n1797: \t\t\t\tBefore: priorVal,\n1798: \t\t\t\tAfter:  proposedNewVal,\n1799: \t\t\t},\n1800: \t\t\tActionReason: reason,\n1801: \t\t}\n1802: \n1803: \t\tplannedNewState := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n1804: \t\t\tValue:  proposedNewVal,\n1805: \t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectPlanned,\n1806: \t\t}\n1807: \n1808: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n1809: \t\t\treturn h.PostDiff(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, plans.Read, priorVal, proposedNewVal)\n1810: \t\t}))\n1811: \n1812: \t\treturn plannedChange, plannedNewState, deferred, keyData, diags\n1813: \t}\n1814: \n1815: \t// We have a complete configuration with no dependencies to wait on, so we\n1816: \t// can read the data source into the state.\n1817: \t// newVal is fully marked by the readDataSource method.\n1818: \tnewVal, readDeferred, readDiags := n.readDataSource(ctx, configVal)\n1819: \n1820: \tif readDeferred != nil {\n1821: \t\t// This will either be null or a value that indicates we're deferred\n1822: \t\t// because of a dependency. In both cases we're happy to just overwrite\n1823: \t\t// that with the more relevant information directly from the provider.\n1824: \t\tdeferred = readDeferred\n1825: \t}\n1826: \n1827: \t// Now we've loaded the data, and diags tells us whether we were successful\n1828: \t// or not, we are going to create our plannedChange and our\n1829: \t// proposedNewState.\n1830: \tvar plannedChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n1831: \tvar plannedNewState *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n1832: \n1833: \t// If we are a nested block, then we want to create a plannedChange that\n1834: \t// tells Terraform to reload the data block during the apply stage even if\n1835: \t// we managed to get the data now.\n1836: \t// Another consideration is that if we failed to load the data, we need to\n1837: \t// disguise that for a nested block. Nested blocks will report the overall\n1838: \t// check as failed but won't affect the rest of the plan operation or block\n1839: \t// an apply operation.\n1840: \n1841: \tif nested {\n1842: \t\taddr := check.Addr().Absolute(n.Addr.Module)\n1843: \n1844: \t\t// Let's fix things up for a nested data block.\n1845: \t\t//\n1846: \t\t// A nested data block doesn't error, and creates a planned change. So,\n1847: \t\t// if we encountered an error we'll tidy up newVal so it makes sense\n1848: \t\t// and handle the error. We'll also create the plannedChange if\n1849: \t\t// appropriate.\n1850: \n1851: \t\tif readDiags.HasErrors() {\n1852: \t\t\t// If we had errors, then we can cover that up by marking the new\n1853: \t\t\t// state as unknown.\n1854: \t\t\tnewVal = objchange.PlannedDataResourceObject(schema, unmarkedConfigVal)\n1855: \n1856: \t\t\t// not only do we want to ensure this synthetic value has the marks,\n1857: \t\t\t// but since this is the value being returned from the data source\n1858: \t\t\t// we need to ensure the schema marks are added as well.\n1859: \t\t\tnewVal = newVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\n1860: \t\t\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(newVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\n1861: \t\t\t\tnewVal = marks.MarkPaths(newVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\n1862: \t\t\t}\n1863: \n1864: \t\t\t// We still want to report the check as failed even if we are still\n1865: \t\t\t// letting it run again during the apply stage.\n1866: \t\t\tctx.Checks().ReportCheckFailure(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0, readDiags.Err().Error())\n1867: \t\t}\n1868: \n1869: \t\t// Any warning or error diagnostics we'll wrap with some special checks\n1870: \t\t// diagnostics. This is so we can identify them later, and so they'll\n1871: \t\t// only report as warnings.\n1872: \t\treadDiags = tfdiags.OverrideAll(readDiags, tfdiags.Warning, func() tfdiags.DiagnosticExtraWrapper {\n1873: \t\t\treturn &addrs.CheckRuleDiagnosticExtra{\n1874: \t\t\t\tCheckRule: addrs.NewCheckRule(addr, addrs.CheckDataResource, 0),\n1875: \t\t\t}\n1876: \t\t})\n1877: \n1878: \t\t// refreshOnly plans cannot produce planned changes, so we only do\n1879: \t\t// this if skipPlanChanges is false. Conversely, provider-deferred data\n1880: \t\t// sources always generate a planned change with a different ActionReason.\n1881: \t\tif !skipPlanChanges && deferred == nil {\n1882: \t\t\tplannedChange = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n1883: \t\t\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n1884: \t\t\t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n1885: \t\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n1886: \t\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n1887: \t\t\t\t\tAction: plans.Read,\n1888: \t\t\t\t\tBefore: priorVal,\n1889: \t\t\t\t\tAfter:  newVal,\n1890: \t\t\t\t},\n1891: \t\t\t\tActionReason: plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseCheckNested,\n1892: \t\t\t}\n1893: \t\t}\n1894: \t}\n1895: \n1896: \t// Provider-deferred data sources always generate a planned change.\n1897: \tif deferred != nil {\n1898: \t\tplannedChange = &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n1899: \t\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n1900: \t\t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n1901: \t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n1902: \t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n1903: \t\t\t\tAction: plans.Read,\n1904: \t\t\t\tBefore: priorVal,\n1905: \t\t\t\tAfter:  newVal,\n1906: \t\t\t},\n1907: \t\t\t// The caller should be more interested in
